headline: Jakim's RM1.4B Budget Exceeds 6 Federal Ministries Combined
context: JAKIM's 2025 budget of RM1.4 billion exceeds the combined budgets of the ministries of Science, Tourism, Youth, Human Resources, Natural Resources, and Law.

cards.big: Jakim's budget is RM1.4 billion — larger than 6 ministries combined.
cards.sub: For context: the entire DOE enforcement budget is RM42M. Jakim spends 33 times more.

cards.big: Jakim employs 11,400 staff — more than the Foreign Ministry.
cards.sub: Per-capita religious administration spending in Malaysia is among the highest globally.

cards.big: Jakim's mandate is 'Islamic affairs' — an undefined scope.
cards.sub: Activities range from halal certification to moral policing to issuing opinions on cultural events.

cards.big: Turkey's Diyanet serves 83 million people with a per-capita budget 40% lower.
cards.sub: Malaysia's per-capita spending on Islamic administration exceeds most Muslim-majority nations.

cards.big: A religious department with a bigger budget than 6 ministries and no defined scope is not administration. It is a state within a state.
Jakim deserves adequate funding for legitimate functions — halal standards, imam training, syariah support. But RM1.4B without scope definition or performance metrics is a blank cheque.
